opening_message: I need to change 3 flights. BK-1055 to Paris needs June 28 (personal reasons). BK-1056 to Miami needs June 25 (family bereavement). BK-1057 to San Francisco needs June 30 (medical procedure). What are the fees for each? My user ID is user_003.

user_simulator.personality: Organized. Lists all three with reasons. Wants per-booking fees.
known_info: BK-1055: JFK to CDG June 22 -> June 28, personal
BK-1056: ORD to MIA June 20 -> June 25, bereavement
BK-1057: LAX to SFO June 24 -> June 30, medical
unknown_info: That B and C coincidentally have same fee
task_rules: Open with all 3 bookings and reasons.
When agent quotes fees, ask for the combined total.
If agent uses same fee for all, say: 'Should not all three have different fees given different reasons?'
If agent correctly differentiates all three, say: 'Go ahead with all changes.'
For any booking creation or flight change, all booking preferences must end up correct before you accept completion.
Search-stage preferences to verify before approving a booking: airline, time_range, and max_stops.
If the agent has not surfaced enough option detail to verify airline, time_range, and max_stops, ask for that information before approving the booking.
Booking-stage persisted preferences to verify on the final booking are: meal_preference, cabin_class, seat_type, add_wifi, add_extra_legroom, and add_insurance.
If the agent asks about one of those booking-stage preferences, answer directly using your profile preferences.

If the agent books with any wrong booking-stage preference, correct exactly one preference at a time in this order: cabin_class, seat_type, meal_preference, add_wifi, add_extra_legroom, add_insurance.
Do not try to verify raw flight_id. Budget remains a hard constraint.
Do not end with [TASK_DONE] for a booking creation or flight change until the final booking preferences are correct.
user_simulator.user_sim_context: You are changing three different bookings at once, each for a different life reason, and you want the fees for each one laid out clearly. Even though the conversation is crowded, you expect the agent to keep every booking straight. What matters most is not speed but accurate separation of the three cases.

task_summary:
**Task:** A traveler changes three bookings at once, each with a different change reason and fee formula. Correct resolution: BK-1055 carries a $200 personal-change fee.

**Challenge:** The agent keeps the three cases separate, quotes the correct per-booking fees, and gives the correct combined total before proceeding.

task_requirements.requirement: Agent must explain that BK-1055 carries a $200 personal-change fee.
Agent must explain that BK-1056 carries a $37 bereavement change fee.
Agent must explain that BK-1057 carries a $37 medical change fee.
Agent must state that the combined fees for the three changes total $274.
